# Sector Swell Monitor — May 2026

*Prototype, generated by hand 2026-05-10. First in the series.*

---

## The bottom line

**The market is warmer than it was. Not a swell yet, but conditions are starting to line up.**

Score this month: **5.7 / 10** — moderate-warm.

---

## What you should do

- **Keep building.** Nothing here forces a decision.
- **One small readiness step:** turn the May acquisition-readiness numbers into a one-page PDF you can send in an hour if needed.
- **One soft outreach:** book one coffee or LinkedIn touch with someone in TKO or DAZN corp dev this quarter. Just a name they recognize later. No pitch.

---

## Why — the three things that got better

**1. UFC is now on broadcast TV and it's working.**
- UFC 326 on CBS pulled 2.5 million viewers in March.
- The next CBS card grew to 2.77 million on the main card.
- Paramount's CEO told analysts the deal is "ahead of expectations."
- Translation: a brand-new mainstream pipe just opened, and it's pumping.

**2. Netflix is entering MMA on May 16.**
- Rousey vs. Carano main event. Ngannou and Diaz on the same card.
- This is a test. If it draws Paul-Tyson level numbers, every streamer pays attention to combat sports.
- Watch the audience number that comes out the week of May 17.

**3. The money is loose.**
- Sports-tech M&A multiples sitting at 11.9× EV/EBITDA — higher than 2019–2021.
- Sovereign and institutional capital flowing into sports.
- DAZN bought Foxtel for $3.4B. Wasserman's boxing arm just rebranded and resold.
- Buyers are paying premium prices for sports assets right now.

---

## Why — the two things that didn't get better

**1. No constellation of stars yet.**
- Topuria is the closest current thing to a breakout. He's defending his title at the White House card June 14.
- He's a real star but he's alone. The Adesanya-era bench depth isn't there.

**2. The buyers are spending elsewhere.**
- TKO posted huge Q1 numbers ($1.59B revenue, +26%) but authorized **$1B more in stock buybacks** instead of doing acquisitions.
- PFL just raised at a $1B valuation — investing inward, not selling.
- DAZN is rolling up boxing rights, not engagement-layer apps.
- Buyers exist. They're just not buying things like us this quarter.

---

## What changed since last month

- Last month I told you the market was soft and UFC fan interest was waning.
- That was right about stars. It was wrong about everything else.
- The CBS ratings are clearer now. The TKO earnings landed May 7. Both are stronger than expected.
- The Netflix card moved from "curiosity" to "near-term test."

---

## Watch these three numbers in the next 60 days

| Date | Signal | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| **May 17** | Netflix MMA debut audience | Tests streamer appetite for live MMA |
| **June 15** | UFC White House card audience | Largest cultural-crossover moment of the year |
| **Early August** | TKO Q2 earnings | Will the buyback signal continue, or shift to M&A? |

If any of those tighten the picture, I'll flag it next month.

---

| Star momentum | 5.0 | flat |
| Media & distribution | 7.5 | up |
| Capital cycle | 6.5 | up |
| Cultural crossover | 7.0 | up |
| Strategic acquirers | 4.0 | down |

**Composite: 5.7** (weighted 25 / 20 / 20 / 15 / 20)

### Methodology

- Lookback: ~60 days (extended for first reading; future months will be 30).
- Sources weighted by reliability. Unverified or tabloid pieces excluded.
- Generated manually as a prototype. Cron build designed in `docs/plans/sector-swell-monitor-2026-05-10.md`.
